reasoning:
The stock closed at 16.40 on February 9. By February 13, it spiked to 18.11 (+10.4%). This massive spike corresponds perfectly with the February 12 earnings report. In Q4 2025 earnings, PCG announced "solid financial results", raised EPS guidance, and doubled the 2026 annualized dividend to $0.20 per share (a major catalyst). 

Post-Earnings Announcement Drift (PEAD) suggests that stocks often continue in the direction of an earnings surprise, especially when accompanied by fundamental improvements like a doubled dividend. The baseline statistical mean-reversion model assigned a 0.39-0.45 probability of staying above 18.11. But the strong fundamental catalyst and ongoing analyst support (price targets at $20-$21) offset the pure technical reversion risk. 

For the first date (Feb 22, about one week out), there is still a moderate risk of temporary profit taking after such an intense short-term spike, so I peg it slightly below 0.5 (at 0.48). Alternatively, momentum can carry it, keeping it hovering near 18.
For the second date (March 17, one month out), PEAD should have more time to manifest or the baseline trend should resume upwards, moving it slightly above 0.5 as PCG stabilizes at the new fundamentally-supported price level.
